macro_segments.title: The Meaning of Holech as Zachi: Interpreting the Agent's Role When Receiving a Get

micro_segments.title: Establishing the Case: Can the Husband Reject the Wife's Agent's Role?
The Gemara's Response: Distinguishing Between Holech and Heilech
The Core Question: Does Holech Mean Immediate Possession (Zachi) or Delivery?

macro_segments.title: Can Both Men and Women Serve as Agents to Deliver or Receive a Get?

micro_segments.title: Obvious Cases: Men as Deliverers, Women as Receivers
The Question: Can Men Receive or Women Deliver?
Proof from Baraita: Women Can Deliver a Get, Including to Themselves

macro_segments.title: When the Agent Misrepresents His Role to the Husband

micro_segments.title: The Setup: Wife Appoints Agent to Deliver; Agent Reports He Should Receive
Rav Nachman's Position: The Husband Relies on What the Agent Says, Not What the Wife Said
Rav Ashi's Objection: Distinguishing Between Two Different Cases
Alternative Explanation: The Agent Is Rejecting His Role as Deliverer

macro_segments.title: Interpreting the Husband's Words Based on Context and Intent

micro_segments.title: When Can We Reinterpret a Person's Words to Make a Transaction Work?
Mistake in Law vs. Mistake in Fact: Different Standards
Case of a Minor Wife: Making the Agent a Shaliach Despite Her Inability to Appoint

macro_segments.title: The Tannaitic Debate on Holech as Zachi in Context

micro_segments.title: Tanna Kamma and Rabbi: Holech Is Zachi When Agent Is Shaliach Lekabala
Rabbi Natan's Position: Holech Never Means Zachi; Only Heilech Does
Baraita: Explicit Distinction Between Holech and Heilech

macro_segments.title: Unresolved Question: Is Holech Zachi When the Wife Already Appointed a Shaliach Lekabala?

micro_segments.title: The Case: Wife's Agent Presents as Shaliach Lekabala; Husband Says Holech V'Ten Lah
Rav Huna's Answer: The Agent Becomes Both His and Hers
Consequence: A Safek Whether She Is a Grusha or Almanah
Analogy: Holech in Money Disputes Also Creates Possession Without Clear Rules

macro_segments.title: Can a Woman Appoint an Agent to Receive a Get from the Husband's Agent?

micro_segments.title: Rav's Position: A Woman Cannot Appoint a Shaliach to Receive from the Husband's Agent
Possible Reason One: Honor of the Husband (Bizayon D'Baal)
Possible Reason Two: Concerns About Indirect Acquisition (Chatzer Haba'ah Me'Achareha)
Rabbi Chanina's Position: A Woman May Appoint Her Own Agent
Key Distinction: Two Separate Agents vs. Transforming One Agent's Identity
